project_structure: Monorepo with three workspace packages (packages/cli, packages/server, packages/shared) managed by pnpm. The CLI package owns install/uninstall behaviour and ships templates/ alongside dist/. Test harness is vitest 3.2.4 (declared as devDependency at root) with per-package vitest.config.ts. The CLI test surface lives under packages/cli/__tests__/ split into three layers: (1) flat unit/contract tests directly in __tests__/ (e.g. hooks-install-validate.test.ts, init-atomic.test.ts, client-configs.test.ts), (2) integration tests under __tests__/integration/ that drive end-to-end install/uninstall through the public execution-plan API (install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts, uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts), (3) shared helpers under __tests__/helpers/ (init-test-utils.ts) plus a real-world cocos-stub fixture at __tests__/fixtures/cocos-stub/ that gets copy-cloned into tmpdir per-test. Snapshot files live at __tests__/__snapshots__/ (4 snapshots: cli-surface, client-configs, i18n, mcp-config-merge). Coverage thresholds in vitest.config.ts are lines:70, statements:70 (v8 provider). Custom coverage gates ship as scripts/rc5-coverage-gate.mjs and scripts/rc6-coverage-gate.mjs.

relevant_files.rationale: Primary integration test exercising the full install flow end-to-end via buildInitExecutionPlan + executeInitExecutionPlan with skipMcp:true. Has 8+ assertion blocks that hard-code the Cursor .cursor/hooks.json shape including events.Stop, events.SessionStart, events.PreToolUse arrays and matcher='Edit|Write|MultiEdit' — every Cursor schema change in rc.14 directly breaks these.
key_symbols: TASK-006 install-skills-and-hooks: Fabric Knowledge Base managed section

key_code.description: Asserts 16 byte-identical artifacts on fresh install across .claude/.codex/.cursor for 3 skills + 3 hook scripts + 3 client configs. Direct JSON.stringify().toContain() probes on cursor events.Stop/SessionStart/PreToolUse arrays — any schema reshape (e.g. wrapping under hooks[] instead of events[]) explodes here.

snapshotTree before/after byte-comparison across .claude and .codex. Cursor is conspicuously NOT snapshotted — gap that rc.14 diff-mode work likely needs to fill so cursor parity is enforced symmetrically.
Seeds a custom Stop hook + permissions block, asserts both survive merge. Pattern to replicate for cursor user-modified scenario tests in diff-mode.

key_code.symbol: managed section idempotency + language change tests
key_code.description: Four it() blocks covering fabric:knowledge-base:begin/end markers, idempotent re-run, fabric_language flip, and user vandalism inside markers. Demonstrates the managed-section semantic which diff-mode behavior likely mirrors.

relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: this single file owns ~90% of install-side Cursor schema coverage. Any rc.14 install fix that touches mergeCursorHookConfig output shape (events.Stop array shape, matcher key, command path resolution) lands its breakage here first. New diff-mode behavior (missing/drift/user-modified detection) needs new describe() blocks added here in the install-skills-and-hooks file.

relevant_files.rationale: Drives installHooks() directly with hand-built fake project trees. Three it() blocks specifically validate cross-client hook path validation including the 'cursor failure' partial-state branch where .cursor is pre-created as a file. The 'happy-path skipped count is exactly 3 fabric-hint + 3 broad-hint + 3 narrow-hint' assertion (L109-L132) is brittle against any rc.14 change that adds or removes a validate step per client.

key_code.description: Asserts skipped[] string contains nine paths: 3 clients × 3 hook scripts (fabric-hint + knowledge-hint-broad + knowledge-hint-narrow). If rc.14 alters hook script count per client or filename, the join(target, '.cursor', 'hooks', 'fabric-hint.cjs') probes mismatch.

Pre-creates .cursor as a regular file (writeFileSync .cursor, 'not a directory'). Asserts result.errors contains 'cursor' OR result.skipped contains '.cursor' + 'hooks.json'. This is the existing 'drift' surface that diff-mode behavior overlaps with.
filter().length === 3 for each hook script suffix. Hard-coded count — adding a 4th hook script or removing one in rc.14 breaks this immediately. Same risk applies to splitting/merging clients.

relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: this file is the focused contract test for the cross-client validation step that fires AFTER mergeCursorHookConfig succeeds. If rc.14 introduces diff-mode (status: 'drift' | 'missing' | 'user-modified'), the InstallHooksResult shape extends, and the simple skipped[].join('\n').contains() assertions need richer matchers.

relevant_files.rationale: Symmetric inverse of install integration test. T1-T6 round-trip cases drive runInit→runUninstall pairs and assert that fabric-owned cursor files are removed (knowledge-hint-broad.cjs, knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs, fabric-hint.cjs). If rc.14 changes the install side, uninstall round-trip tests must update in lockstep because they assert the exact filenames that install wrote.

key_code.description: Asserts cursor-owned files are removed by name (knowledge-hint-broad.cjs, knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs). Note: explicitly snapshots only .claude tree — cursor tree is NOT snapshotted, parallel asymmetry to install test.
Verifies CLAUDE.md begin/end markers are stripped and user content survives. Diff-mode for managed sections likely lands here.

relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: rc.14 fixes are symmetric (install + cursor hooks); uninstall must preserve symmetry. Any new install-side behavior (e.g. mark-as-managed metadata) needs a matching uninstall test to assert clean reversal.

relevant_files.rationale: Shared test infrastructure: createWerewolfFixtureRoot copies the cocos-stub fixture into tmpdir-per-test, scrubbing AGENTS.md/.fabric/.claude. Also exposes writeFixtureFile, readFixtureFile, createEmptyFixtureRoot, setProcessTty. EVERY install/uninstall test file imports from here — modifying this helper to support diff-mode pre-seeding (e.g. seed with stale hooks.json, seed with user-modified hook script) is the right extension point.

key_code.description: Copies __tests__/fixtures/cocos-stub/ recursively to tmpdir/{prefix}-{pid}-{ts}-{rand}, then removes AGENTS.md / .fabric / .claude so the fixture starts from 'project exists, fabric not yet initialized'. Does NOT scrub .cursor — important because cocos-stub does not ship .cursor, so install tests see a fresh-cursor scenario.

Pre-seeds arbitrary content at a relative path inside the fixture root, used to simulate 'already-init' scenarios (e.g. user pre-creates .claude/settings.json before install runs). Pattern for diff-mode 'user-modified' test setup.

relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: this is the single point where rc.14 diff-mode tests will need new helpers (e.g. seedDriftedCursorHooks, seedUserModifiedHookScript) so each diff-mode scenario does not have to hand-roll fs manipulation.

relevant_files.rationale: Canonical 'a real cocos-creator-flavoured TypeScript project that hasn't been fab init'd' fixture. Contains AGENTS.md (stripped by helper), package.json, project.config.json, README.md, tsconfig.json, assets/scripts/. Used by EVERY install/uninstall test as the baseline workspace.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: any rc.14 diff-mode test that needs to test 'install ran previously, now we re-run with drift' will base off this fixture. Note the fixture currently has zero .claude/.cursor/.codex prefix — adding pre-seeded versions of the fixture is one option for diff-mode setup.

relevant_files.rationale: Atomic-write contract test for init scaffold. Three describe() blocks cover .tmp file residue, events.jsonl raw-write contract, and atomicWriteJson spy assertions. Likely UNAFFECTED by rc.14 cursor changes (init scaffold is filesystem-shape contract, not client-config-shape). Listed as context_only — but should re-run as regression safety net.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: regression safety net for the atomic-write substrate that mergeCursorHookConfig depends on (atomicWriteJson under the hood). If rc.14 changes the write substrate, these break first.

relevant_files.rationale: Covers --scope routing (project vs user) and MCP config deep-merge preserving existing entries. Tests writeClaudeMcpConfig directly. Not directly affected by rc.14 install/cursor-hook fixes — but is a pattern reference for 'how existing-entry merge is tested' which diff-mode merge behavior may want to mirror.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: demonstrates the existing pattern for 'pre-seed config + assert merge preserves user keys' which is the same pattern diff-mode 'user-modified detection' tests should follow.

relevant_files.rationale: Init guard / preflight test (covered by grep cursor hits in install-related set). Likely tests non-destructive guards and abort-existing semantics that planFreshPath relies on. Listed as context-only since rc.14 task hits install but may not change guard logic.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: planFreshPath callsite verification; if rc.14 reshapes the force/reapply contract, this file's assertions are second-line breakage.

relevant_files.rationale: Unit/command-surface tests for fab uninstall covering plan enumeration, --plan no-write, --purge HOME isolation, idempotency, --force, and wizard cancellation banner. Mirrors the install pattern but at unit level. New uninstall behavior in rc.14 (if symmetric) lands here.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: companion to install-cli-surface.test.ts. If rc.14 fixes touch the uninstall command surface (e.g. new --diff flag, status: drift output), assertion sites land here.

relevant_files.rationale: Unit tests for the install CLI surface: --reapply canonical forceful rerun, --plan no-write, --reapply over existing init, fabric-config.json scaffold preservation, retired-sentinel-prompt absence. Drives installCommand.run() with stubbed clack prompts. Covers planFreshPath behavior indirectly via reapply tests.

key_code.symbol: reapply as canonical forceful rerun it() block
key_code.description: Asserts plan.options.force === true AND plan.options.reapply === true — exact pairing semantics. If rc.14 changes how reapply maps to force (or decouples them for diff-mode), this breaks.

key_code.symbol: reapply over existing init it() block
key_code.description: Runs install twice with reapply on second run, asserts legacy bootstrap/README.md is PRESERVED while v2.0 layout exists. Directly probes planFreshPath / managed-vs-unmanaged distinction.

relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: covers planFreshPath behavior on the command-surface side. rc.14 diff-mode for 'missing/drift/user-modified' likely needs new it() blocks here mirroring the reapply pattern.

relevant_files.rationale: Snapshot-based drift gate for the public command surface (install, scan, doctor, serve, uninstall args). cli-surface.test.ts.snap is the source of truth for every flag. If rc.14 adds a new --diff or --dry-run flag to install/uninstall, this snapshot WILL fail and must be updated with pnpm test -u along with docs/test-seed/cli.md §1.

key_code.description: Snapshot loop over [install, scan, doctor, serve, uninstall]. Each command's flag set is serialized and toMatchSnapshot'd with DRIFT_HINT appended. New flags added in rc.14 trigger this gate first.

relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: this is the first failure surface for any new CLI flag introduced by rc.14 (e.g. --check-drift, --diff, --print-plan-only). Update path is documented in the snapshot hint itself.

relevant_files.rationale: Snapshot file enumerating every flag of install/uninstall/scan/doctor/serve commands. Direct source-of-truth that must be regenerated with pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -u when rc.14 changes CLI surface.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: lockstep update target alongside cli-surface.test.ts. Must NOT be hand-edited; regenerate via -u.

relevant_files.rationale: Snapshot for client-configs.test.ts (Claude .mcp.json, Codex codex.toml mcp_servers, Cursor .cursor/mcp.json). Note: this test snapshots the MCP config serialization, NOT the Cursor hooks.json schema. Listed because the grep hit was on the test file, but actual coverage is MCP-side; rc.14 cursor-hooks changes likely do not touch this snapshot.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: scoped to MCP config emission, NOT cursor hook config. Probably untouched by rc.14 unless mcp.json changes too.

relevant_files.rationale: Golden snapshot tests for client mcp.json/codex.toml/.cursor/mcp.json emission. Covers writeClaudeMcpConfig and writeJsonClientConfig (Cursor MCP path). Scoped to MCP servers, NOT hooks. rc.14 should not touch this unless cursor MCP shape also changes.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: edge of the cursor-related test surface — clarifies scope. If rc.14 stays inside cursor hook config, this stays untouched.

relevant_files.rationale: Integration test for atomic-write substrate. Listed because it's part of the install-adjacent test set. Likely covers the write primitive used by mergeCursorHookConfig and other JSON mergers. Regression-only relevance for rc.14.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: substrate regression net. Unlikely to need updates but should be run.

relevant_files.rationale: Source-of-truth for the install logic the test files exercise: mergeCursorHookConfig (L476-L488), copyTextIdempotent (L586-L604), mergeJsonIdempotent (L606-L620), addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection (L513-L580). Tests treat this module as a black box via the public exports. Cursor hook fix in rc.14 lands here; tests that probe the result must match the new shape.

key_code.description: Reads templates/hooks/configs/cursor-hooks.json, deep-merges into projectRoot/.cursor/hooks.json with array-append-dedupe over events.Stop, events.SessionStart, events.PreToolUse. Returns InstallStepResult with step='cursor-hook-config'. This is the function rc.14's cursor-hooks fix likely modifies.

Per-client array paths array. Cursor uses [events.Stop, events.SessionStart, events.PreToolUse]. Schema-shape contract that tests probe via JSON.stringify().toContain() in install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts.

relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: target module whose behavior the test suite encodes. Any rc.14 change here cascades into install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts and hooks-install-validate.test.ts.

relevant_files.rationale: Hosts planFreshPath() (L1020-L1030) — the function the task description calls out by name. Also owns buildInitExecutionPlan, executeInitExecutionPlan, runInitCommand, installCommand. planFreshPath returns 'created' | 'overwritten' and THROWS abort-existing without --force. rc.14 diff-mode likely needs this to return a richer enum ('drift' | 'missing' | 'user-modified').

key_code.description: Binary disposition only: returns 'created' if absent, 'overwritten' if present + force, else THROWS. No drift detection. Used at L641-L643 for agents.meta.json / events.jsonl / forensic.json planning. rc.14 diff-mode extension target.

relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: drives the test pattern in install-cli-surface.test.ts (reapply behavior) and init-atomic.test.ts (.tmp residue assertions). Any diff-mode return type change cascades into these tests.

relevant_files.rationale: The template fragment that mergeCursorHookConfig deep-merges into .cursor/hooks.json. Current shape: events.Stop[], events.SessionStart[], events.PreToolUse[{matcher: 'Edit|Write|MultiEdit', command: '.cursor/hooks/knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs'}]. Tests assert this exact shape — any rc.14 schema change is reflected here AND in the install integration test in lockstep.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: schema source of truth. If rc.14 fixes the Cursor hook config shape (e.g. wraps events under hooks{} or changes matcher semantics), this file changes and the test assertions in install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L208-L221 must update to match.

relevant_files.rationale: Per-package vitest config: include pattern is __tests__/**/*.test.ts, environment is node, coverage provider v8 with thresholds lines:70 statements:70, excludes src/**/*.test.ts and types/**. Path aliases route @fenglimg/fabric-server and @fenglimg/fabric-shared to src/ for local resolution. New diff-mode source files added in rc.14 are auto-included for coverage tracking.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: defines coverage thresholds the rc.14 work must satisfy (lines >= 70%). New diff-mode source files need accompanying tests to avoid threshold regression.

relevant_files.rationale: Defines test scripts: `test: vitest run` and `test:coverage: vitest run --coverage`. Pinned to vitest via root devDependency (^3.2.4). No jest, no other test runner. Confirms 'what test framework is used' question 1.
relevant_files.topic_relation: test-surface: confirms vitest 3.2.4 as the test runner. No conditional skip patterns; describe.skipIf used inline (e.g. POSIX-only test L329 of install-skills-and-hooks).

patterns: Test patterns observed across the install/uninstall surface: (1) tmpdir-based fixture cloning — every integration test calls createWerewolfFixtureRoot(prefix) which copies __tests__/fixtures/cocos-stub recursively into os.tmpdir(); tempRoots[] array drained in afterEach via cleanupFixtureRoot. (2) Module-scoped tempRoots[] + originalHome / originalFabricHome env capture-restore at module scope (see uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L41-L60). (3) runInit/runUninstall wrappers that hardcode skipMcp:true so tests don't try to install MCP server globally. (4) snapshotTree() helper duplicated in each integration test (NOT yet hoisted to helpers/) — builds {relativePath: contentString} for byte-comparison. (5) describe.skipIf(process.platform === 'win32') for POSIX-only assertions (exec-bit checks). (6) toMatchSnapshot() for command surface drift + client config emission only — install integration tests use explicit JSON.stringify().toContain() rather than snapshots so diffs are readable. (7) Hooks idempotency tests run install twice with reapply+force and assert snapshot equality before==after. (8) installHooks() is driven via direct call in unit tests, while integration tests drive it indirectly via runInit. (9) writeFixtureFile pre-seeds 'already-init' state to test merge/preserve behavior. (10) Pre-creating .claude or .cursor as a regular file (not directory) is the canonical pattern for testing partial-install resilience (install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L416, hooks-install-validate.test.ts L89). (11) NO mocking of fs in integration tests — all real-fs against tmpdir. Mocking confined to clack/prompts and vi.spyOn(atomicWriteModule) for write-substrate assertion in init-atomic.test.ts. (12) i18n locale pinning via vi.hoisted(() => { process.env.FAB_LANG = 'en'; }) before command imports in cli-surface.test.ts.
dependencies: Test runner: vitest ^3.2.4 (root devDependency). Coverage: @vitest/coverage-v8 ^3.2.4 (root devDependency), v8 provider in vitest.config.ts. Per-test deps: node:fs (statSync, readFileSync, cpSync, mkdtempSync, rmSync, writeFileSync), node:path (join, dirname, resolve), node:os (tmpdir), node:url (fileURLToPath). Internal imports: ../src/commands/install.ts (buildInitExecutionPlan, executeInitExecutionPlan, initFabric, installCommand, runInitCommand), ../src/commands/uninstall.ts (buildUninstallExecutionPlan, executeUninstallExecutionPlan), ../src/commands/hooks.ts (installHooks), helpers/init-test-utils.ts (createWerewolfFixtureRoot, cleanupFixtureRoot, writeFixtureFile, readFixtureFile, setProcessTty). Path aliases (vitest.config.ts): @fenglimg/fabric-server → packages/server/src/index.ts, @fenglimg/fabric-shared* → packages/shared/src/*. Coverage gates: scripts/rc5-coverage-gate.mjs and scripts/rc6-coverage-gate.mjs (custom thresholds beyond vitest.config.ts). Lefthook git hooks + knip dependency analysis run in CI alongside vitest. No jest, no mocha, no playwright in CLI test surface.
integration_points: Test entrypoints (where rc.14 changes hit existing test assertions): packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:L113 (fresh init schema probes — 16 artifact byte-checks), L208-L221 (cursor events.Stop/SessionStart/PreToolUse + Edit|Write|MultiEdit matcher), L240-L243 (idempotency snapshotTree byte-equality), L295-L322 (dedup assertions on hooks.Stop count), L382-L394 (skipped >= 18 count from installHooks post-init). packages/cli/__tests__/hooks-install-validate.test.ts:L48-L67 (cross-client validate happy-path with 9 hook script path probes), L89-L101 (cursor partial-state branch), L114-L131 (exact triple count per hook filename). packages/cli/__tests__/integration/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:L124-L151 (cursor + .claude file removal assertions), L296-L303 (idempotent re-run 100%-skipped). packages/cli/__tests__/__snapshots__/cli-surface.test.ts.snap (regenerate on any flag change). packages/cli/__tests__/install-cli-surface.test.ts:L46-L61 (reapply pairing semantics). NEW test entry-points needed for rc.14 diff-mode: (a) describe('diff-mode: missing scenarios') — install hook script deleted between runs, install should detect missing and reinstall. (b) describe('diff-mode: drift scenarios') — hook script content modified, install should detect drift via hash and (re-write|preserve|prompt). (c) describe('diff-mode: user-modified detection') — .cursor/hooks.json hand-edited, install should detect user keys vs managed keys and preserve user. New helper needed in __tests__/helpers/init-test-utils.ts: seedPartialInstall(target, opts: {missingScript?: string; driftedScript?: string; userModifiedConfig?: object}) to factor the pre-seed complexity out of every diff-mode test.
constraints: Coverage thresholds: lines:70, statements:70 in packages/cli/vitest.config.ts — new diff-mode source code must reach 70%+ or threshold fails CI. Some tests are POSIX-only (describe.skipIf(process.platform === 'win32')) — exec-bit checks for hook scripts. Snapshot tests live at __tests__/__snapshots__/ (4 files: cli-surface, client-configs, i18n, mcp-config-merge); any flag/schema change requires `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -u` to regenerate AND a docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 update per the DRIFT_HINT in cli-surface.test.ts L19-L24. snapshotTree() helper is currently duplicated across install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts and uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts — refactoring it into helpers/init-test-utils.ts is a clean-slate opportunity (memory: pre-user clean-slate preference). NO existing cursor-tree snapshot in idempotency tests (install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L240-L243 only snapshots .claude + .codex, deliberately omitting .cursor) — this is a coverage gap that rc.14 work should fill to enforce cursor parity symmetrically. Custom rc5/rc6 coverage gates (scripts/rc5-coverage-gate.mjs, scripts/rc6-coverage-gate.mjs) may target specific files/symbols; if rc.14 adds source files matching their patterns, gate thresholds need updating. No e2e/browser tests in CLI package — all real-fs + tmpdir against the cocos-stub fixture. test:coverage script ships at both root + packages/cli; CI runs pnpm -r --if-present test:coverage.

clarification_needs.question: Should rc.14 diff-mode introduce a new result status (e.g. 'drift' | 'missing' | 'user-modified') on InstallStepResult, or piggyback on existing 'skipped' status with richer message strings?
clarification_needs.context: Current InstallStepResult shape exposes status: 'written' | 'skipped' | 'error' (used in install-skills-and-hooks.ts and asserted via .length / .filter in hooks-install-validate.test.ts). Adding new enum values is more discoverable but breaks every existing test that does `result.skipped.length` or `result.skipped.filter(...)`. The hooks-install-validate.test.ts exact-count assertions (L114-L131) are particularly brittle here.
options: Extend InstallStepResult status enum with 'drift' / 'missing-managed' — typed, discoverable, but breaks existing skipped-count assertions and requires test-wide refactor
Keep ternary status, encode diff-mode via message string prefix (e.g. 'drift:hook-script-modified') — backwards-compatible but stringly-typed
Introduce a sibling InstallStepDetail object alongside status — additive, non-breaking, but doubles result shape complexity

clarification_needs.question: Should diff-mode tests live as new describe() blocks inside the existing install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts, or as a NEW dedicated file (e.g. install-diff-mode.test.ts)?
clarification_needs.context: install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts is already 580 LOC with 8 describe blocks (fresh init, idempotency, settings preservation, dedup, POSIX exec bit, fabric-hooks idempotent, partial install, managed section). Adding 3-4 more diff-mode blocks pushes it past 800 LOC and dilutes the per-rc traceability tags currently in describe names (rc.2-rc.12).
options: New file install-diff-mode.test.ts — clean separation, easy to grep for rc.14 work, parallel test isolation, but duplicates the runInit / snapshotTree helpers (third copy)
Extend install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts with rc.14 describe blocks — keeps install surface centralized, but file balloons past 800 LOC
New file PLUS hoist snapshotTree + runInit into helpers/init-test-utils.ts — most invasive but pays down accumulated helper duplication debt

clarification_needs.question: Does rc.14 need to extend the cocos-stub fixture with PRE-INITIALIZED variants (e.g. cocos-stub-initialized-clean, cocos-stub-initialized-drifted) or should each diff-mode test compose pre-init state via writeFixtureFile helpers?
clarification_needs.context: Current fixture is 'clean uninstalled' state. Diff-mode tests need 'already-installed' starting points. Two patterns: shipped pre-init fixture variants (deterministic, easy to inspect, but proliferate maintenance burden if templates evolve) vs. compositional pre-seeding (calls writeFixtureFile + manual JSON construction in every test — repetitive but mirrors actual install template output).
options: Compose pre-init state via writeFixtureFile + a new seedPostInstallState helper in init-test-utils.ts — single source of truth, evolves with templates
Ship a second fixture cocos-stub-installed/ under __tests__/fixtures/ — fast to use but drifts from real install output if templates change without fixture refresh
Run actual runInit() at the start of each diff-mode test then mutate from there — slowest but always faithful to real install output

_metadata.task_description: rc.14 contains two fixes that touch install + cursor hooks. Need to map test coverage to understand which tests will need updating, which new tests are required for diff-mode behavior, and what test infrastructure exists.
